source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to BOP Lower Gripper Assembly

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Garden Banks Block 427

event.top_event: Hand Crush Injury During BOP Maintenance
actions_taken: Immediate assistance called by Noble Subsea Engineer
Attempt to raise gripper assembly to free hand
Hydraulic hand pump used to raise landing plate 2.5 inches
Medical attention provided by Rig Medic
Helicopter evacuation for onshore medical evaluation
Surgery performed to remove four fingers and part of thumb
event.summary: On 29 May 2017, a third-party SubC technician sustained a serious crush injury to his left hand while adjusting Belleville springs on a subsea BOP lower gripper assembly aboard the Noble Don Taylor drill ship. The technician placed his left hand in a gap between the connector plate and landing sleeve for anchoring while using a ratchet extension with his right hand. When the Noble Subsea Engineer actuated the gripper to unlatch and block positions, the connector plate fell and trapped the technician's hand against the landing sleeve. A hydraulic hand pump was used to free the hand. The technician was evacuated by helicopter and required surgery resulting in amputation of four fingers and part of his thumb.
recommendations: BSEE Lafayette District made no formal recommendations to the Office of Incident Investigations

hazards.name: Pressurized Hydraulic System in BOP Gripper Assembly
Moving Mechanical Components - Connector Plate and Landing Sleeve
Pinch Point Between Upper and Lower BOP Assemblies

threats.name: Improper Hand Placement in Pinch Point During Maintenance
Unintended Gripper Actuation While Technician Hand in Gap
Connector Plate Misalignment Creating Unexpected Gap

consequences.name: Amputation of Fingers and Partial Thumb
Evacuation and Requirement for Emergency Surgery

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and safe work procedures prior to task execution
controls.lod_basis: Pre-job hazard identification and control planning

supporting_text: the task of guiding the lower and upper assemblies with hands is not referenced in the NOV OEM procedures and was not addressed as a 'pinch point' hazard in the JSA
inadequate pre-job preparation and job hazardous analysis

controls.name: NOV Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Provide correct operational and maintenance procedures for BOP gripper assembly
controls.lod_basis: Equipment manufacturer technical documentation and procedures

supporting_text: there was some confusion about the gripper assembly functions and with the NOV original equipment manufacturer (OEM) procedures by both parties
the task of guiding the lower and upper assemblies with hands is not referenced in the NOV OEM procedures

controls.name: Communication Protocol Between Subsea Team Members
controls.barrier_role: Ensure clear coordination and understanding of task steps and hazards between team members
controls.lod_basis: Work coordination and safety communication procedures

supporting_text: both parties failed to clearly communicate with each other during the execution of job task
poor communication between the Noble Subsea Engineer and SubC Technician during the job task

controls.name: Supervision and Work Oversight
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safe work practices and hazard awareness during maintenance operations
controls.lod_basis: Management oversight and regulatory requirement for safe operations

supporting_text: Shell failed to provide adequate supervision when a SubC third-party service technician sustained a serious injury to his left hand while working on a BOP blue pod lower gripper assembly

controls.name: Emergency Response and Hand Pump Assistance
controls.barrier_role: Rapidly free trapped hand and provide immediate medical care
controls.lod_basis: Emergency equipment and immediate response procedures

supporting_text: The Noble Subsea Engineer who witnessed the incident immediately called for assistance and attempted to raise the gripper assembly to free the SubC Technician's left hand
a hydraulic hand pump was used to raise the landing plate approximately 2.5 inches to free his left hand

controls.name: Medical Evacuation and Onshore Treatment
controls.barrier_role: Provide emergency medical care and surgical intervention for serious injury
controls.lod_basis: Emergency medical response and helicopter evacuation procedures

supporting_text: He was escorted to the Rig Medic's office for medical attention and then evacuated by helicopter the same day from the rig for medical evaluation by a physician onshore

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
